checks.finding: All four elements present. (1) Metric row named: Target-Metric: stale-narrative-vs-current-state Minor findings per feature, recorded in quality verification gate reports (count). (2) Baseline and target: Baseline: 2 (evidence-deep-verify, 2026-07-09); Target: <=1. (3) Cycle count: Horizon: next completed feature retrospective (one cycle). (4) Threshold: 'the proposal succeeds only if that count is <=1 for the feature and every implementation report generated in that feature carries the new Snapshot Notice line from its first commit', plus a secondary check that no gate report shows a reviewer editing a frozen implementation report. No missing element.

Evidence scale: widespread and recurring -- the 4th consecutive full-track feature with this finding class (evidence-deep-verify 2 instances this period; ci-mcp 4; sdd-domain FP-02, 6 occurrences across 5 tasks; local-env-mcp 3). Change scope: two coordinated additive edits that address both halves of the identified root cause -- a Snapshot Notice paragraph in the report template (disclosing commit-scoping) and a matching reconciliation instruction added to the gate skill's finding-classification step (directing reviewers to record current values in the gate report rather than editing the frozen implementation report). This is not a single-sentence patch; it is a two-sided fix sized to a persistent, root-cause-identified friction pattern. Proportional.

Scanned docs/workflow-improvements/ for Status: Verified WFIs: WFI-001, WFI-002, WFI-003, WFI-004 (all Verified). All four Proposed Change tables target only AGENTS.md subsections (high-risk-task preflight; review-gate precheck fallback; evidence report identity fields; post-review artifact freeze / provenance re-review). None touch plugins/sdd-implementation/templates/implementation-report.template.md or plugins/sdd-quality-loop/skills/quality-gate/SKILL.md, the two files WFI-006 targets. No overlap, no conflict. (WFI-005 also touches the quality-loop plugin's template/policy surface but is Status: Applied, not Verified, and is excluded from this check by definition; its own targets -- quality-report.template.md's Feature line and deterministic-check-policy.md's placeholder-scan waiver text -- are disjoint from WFI-006's Snapshot Notice and finding-classification-step edits in any case.)

Category is plugin-improvement (correctly classified per the guide's flowchart: cross-plugin handoff friction between the implementation-phase report and the quality verification gate's independent review). The WFI declares zero direct project-side Proposed Change ('WFI Direct Target (project-side): None') and routes the entire fix through the sanctioned GitHub-Issue lane (category guide Section 1: 'A GitHub Issue will be created after audit completion'), which is the designed mechanism for this category, not a misclassification. The root cause -- neither the report template nor the gate skill's own process text state the commit-scoped nature of report data or provide a reconciliation instruction -- lives inside the two plugin files themselves; an AGENTS.md-only substitute could not reach the specific template heading or the gate skill's finding-classification step. Confirmed via `git diff origin/main -- plugins/`: both edits are applied (7-line Snapshot Notice block in implementation-report.template.md; 6-line reconciliation instruction added to quality-gate/SKILL.md step 9), demonstrating the fix is concretely achievable through the declared mechanism.

Scanned Root Cause Hypothesis, Proposed Change Change Description column, and Expected Effect against every forbidden term in wfi-category-guide.md Section 2. The only matches for `quality-gate`/`sdd-quality-loop` are inside the Target File column (`plugins/sdd-quality-loop/skills/quality-gate/SKILL.md`), a literal path citation, matching the WFI-005 precedent that path citations in the Target File column are not prose usage. Prose elsewhere consistently uses the required generic substitutions ('quality verification gate', 'implementation phase', 'gate process', 'independent gate reviewer'). No forbidden term newly introduced by the Cycle 1 restructuring (the new 'WFI Direct Target' / 'GitHub-Issue Lane' subheadings and their surrounding prose use only 'plugins/' as a generic directory noun, WFI-004 as a cross-reference, and wfi-category-guide.md section citations -- none are forbidden terms).

Target is <=1 stale-narrative finding next feature, against a current-period baseline of 2. The friction mechanism is a documentation/process gap (no commit-scoping disclosure, no reconciliation instruction), and the proposed change patches both gaps directly and additively. Reducing from 2 to <=1 is a modest, not implausibly optimistic, target given the fix addresses the identified root cause structurally. No advisory needed.

Category: plugin-improvement, so this check applies. Problem Evidence summarizes cleanly in 2-3 generic sentences (recurring stale-narrative findings across 4 consecutive features). The GitHub-Issue Lane Proposed Change table has 2 rows with concrete, generic, non-vague Change Description text sufficient to populate the Section 4 issue template verbatim. Expected Effect states a quantitative target (2 to <=1) plus an explicit non-decreasing-guard statement. No row would produce an empty or vague issue body.

Meta-Change is not set; the strict lane does not apply. The field is explicitly declared `Meta-Change: false` (not absent), so the absent-field override does not trigger. Noted for the record: the added gate-skill instruction ('classify the finding Accepted' for stale-narrative divergence) touches the finding-classification step of the quality verification gate, which is adjacent to audit logic; however it standardizes an already-occurring ad hoc reviewer resolution (T-004/T-007) rather than introducing, loosening, or removing any threshold, check, or classification option, so re-scrutiny under the strict lane would not change the outcome even if it applied.
